_doc: Error logs for the faulting job. The robot-service entry explicitly attributes the fault to a session-creation TIMEOUT (the create-session call did not complete within the 120s timeout) and notes LSA returned NO logon rejection — the defining contrast with a logon-failure (job-faulted-logon-failure). It also records Robot version 23.4.7, corroborating the < 23.10 known defect.

Data.Message:
Job 'c0ffee01-1111-4222-8333-444455556666' failed to start. Could not start executor. Creating user session timed out.
   at UiPath.Robot.Service.SessionManager.CreateSessionAsync(LogonRequest request)
   at UiPath.Robot.Service.JobManager.StartJobAsync(JobStartRequest request, CancellationToken cancellationToken)

[Robot] Creating a Windows session for UIPATH\SVC_RECON on RECON-BOT-01 did not complete within the 120s session-creation timeout. The create-session call timed out; LSA returned NO logon rejection (no bad-password, locked, or expired status). Robot version 23.4.7.
